response: Karen packs peanut butter sandwiches on 2 randomly chosen days of the week, ham sandwiches on 3 days, cake on 1 randomly chosen day, and cookies on the other 4 days. The probability of a ham sandwich and cake being on the same day is calculated by considering the overlap of these events. The total number of days is 7. The probability of a ham sandwich is 3/7, and the probability of cake is 1/7. The probability of both events occurring on the same day is (3/7) * (1/7) = 3/49. Converting this to a percentage, it is approximately 6.12%. Many days have both ham and cake, but only one day has cake. The final answer is 6.12%.
